2604.02863.title: EMS: Multi-Agent Voting via Efficient Majority-then-Stopping
2604.02863.paper_summary: Majority voting is the standard for aggregating multi-agent responses into a final decision. However, traditional methods typically require all agents to complete their reasoning before aggregation begins, leading to significant computational overhead, as many responses become redundant once a majority consensus is achieved. In this work, we formulate the multi-agent voting as a reliability-aware agent scheduling problem, and propose an Efficient Majority-then-Stopping (EMS) to improve reasoning efficiency. EMS prioritizes agents based on task-aware reliability and terminates the reasoning pipeline the moment a majority is achieved from the following three critical components. Specifically, we introduce Agent Confidence Modeling (ACM) to estimate agent reliability using historical performance and semantic similarity, Adaptive Incremental Voting (AIV) to sequentially select agents with early stopping, and Individual Confidence Updating (ICU) to dynamically update the reliability of each contributing agent. Extensive evaluations across six benchmarks demonstrate that EMS consistently reduces the average number of invoked agents by 32%.
2604.02863.paper_summary_zh: 主要投票是將多代理回應聚合為最終決策的標準。<lb/>然而，傳統方法通常要求所有代理在聚合開始之前完成推理，這導致了顯著的計算開銷，因為一旦達成多數共識，許多回應便變得冗餘。<lb/>在本研究中，我們將多代理投票構建為一個考慮可靠性的代理調度問題，並提出了一種高效的多數-然後-停止（EMS）方法來提高推理效率。<lb/>EMS根據任務相關的可靠性來優先考慮代理，並在達成多數的瞬間終止推理流程，這基於以下三個關鍵組件。<lb/>具體來說，我們引入了代理信心建模（ACM）來利用歷史表現和語義相似性來估計代理的可靠性，適應性增量投票（AIV）用於順序選擇代理並進行提前停止，以及個體信心更新（ICU）用於動態更新每個貢獻代理的可靠性。<lb/>在六個基準上的廣泛評估顯示，EMS始終將被調用的代理的平均數量減少了32%。
2604.02863.author: Yiqing Liu et.al.
2604.02863.authors: Yiqing Liu, Hantao Yao, Wu Liu, Yongdong Zhang
